reforms.description: The Czech Republic is continuing the 'Education for Competitiveness' programme from 2014 to 2020, which enhances collaboration between enterprises and higher education institutions (HEIs) in innovation and research. Additionally, the IPN Methodology project aims to create a new system for evaluating and financing research, development, and innovation (R&D&I). The government also adopted the Act on Child Group in January 2014 to reduce obstacles for establishing not-for-profit public and private childcare providers, thereby expanding childcare provision.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between enterprises and HEIs fosters innovation and research, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act on Child Group was adopted and the Education for Competitiveness programme is ongoing.
reforms.importance_rationale: The continuation of the programme and the introduction of new systems for evaluation and financing represent significant but not structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: In January 2014, the government adopted the Act on Child Group, which should significantly decrease obstacles to the establishment of not-for-profit public and private providers.

reforms.description: An amendment to the Income Tax Act was introduced to boost tax deductibility for R&D projects, increasing the tax deduction from 100% to 110% if R&D expenditures increase compared to the previous tax period.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing tax deductibility for R&D projects incentivizes private sector investment in innovation, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The amendment to the Income Tax Act has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The amendment represents a significant change in the tax treatment of R&D expenditures, encouraging more investment.
reforms.source_quote: In 2011, an amendment of Income Tax Act boosted tax deductibility to include services related to the realization of R&D projects.

reforms.description: The Czech Republic has implemented reforms to reduce regulatory and administrative barriers for starting businesses, including a significant cut in the minimum capital requirement for new enterprises from CZK200,000 to CZK1.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing barriers to starting businesses encourages entrepreneurship and innovation, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant reduction in the minimum capital requirement represents a structural change in the business environment.
reforms.source_quote: The minimum capital requirement was reduced from CZK200,000 to CZK1.

reforms.description: The Czech government plans to implement a youth guarantee in 2014 aimed at reducing youth unemployment by providing better career guidance, facilitating participation in active labor market policies, and improving mechanisms for young people to return to formal education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving career guidance and facilitating access to education for youth enhances human capital, which is essential for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan for the youth guarantee has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The youth guarantee represents a significant policy initiative aimed at addressing youth unemployment and enhancing skills.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to implement a youth guarantee in 2014 to reduce the youth unemployment rate.

reforms.description: The Czech government amended the law on corporate taxation to introduce new tax-deductible items for workplace training and assets purchased for training, effective from January 1, 2014.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Introducing tax-deductible items for workplace training encourages firms to invest in employee development, which can enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The amendment to the law on corporate taxation has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The amendment represents a significant change in the tax treatment of training expenses, promoting investment in human capital.
reforms.source_quote: The government has amended the law on corporate taxation, introducing two new tax-deductible items for training in the workplace.
